title: xtra-Energizing German Soybean-Sprout-Kongnamul Pilaf
description: A diabetic-friendly, high-protein German dish featuring soybean sprouts, kongnamul, and shio-cure, perfect for breakfast or lunch.
instructions: In a large saucepan, melt the unsalted butter over medium heat.
Add the chopped scallions and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the soybean sprouts and kongnamul; cook for 4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the quinoa and cook for 1 minute.
Add the shio-cured salmon fillet, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Pour in the unsweetened almond milk and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 15 minutes or until the liquid has been absorbed.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and season with salt and pepper to taste.
